syntax_8541.creator: Poa
syntax_8541.title: Fake level
syntax_8541.description: Make a player see their XP level as something that it is not

en: Make a player see their XP level as something that it is not

syntax_13306.creator: Poa
syntax_13306.title: Bukkit Item Stack
syntax_13306.description: Gets the bukkit Item Stack class version of an item

en: Gets the bukkit Item Stack class version of an item
Remove a created guardian beam

syntax_11949.creator: Poa
syntax_11949.title: Set/Change Player Vision
syntax_11949.description:
Changes the vision to either **Creeper, Enderman, Spider or Player**

These are the only supported options.

 *note*
This effect is based on a Minecraft bug (https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MC-46503)
The support for this depends on how long it takes Minecraft to fix this :D

en:
Changes the vision to either **Creeper, Enderman, Spider or Player**

These are the only supported options.

 *note*
This effect is based on a Minecraft bug (https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MC-46503)
The support for this depends on how long it takes Minecraft to fix this :D
Turn the client glow off

syntax_8536.creator: Poa
syntax_8536.title: Server Brand
syntax_8536.description: Set the server brand in the player's f3 page

en: Set the server brand in the player's f3 page

syntax_8537.creator: Poa
syntax_8537.title: Set IP
syntax_8537.syntax_pattern: set ip of %player% to %string% with port %number%

en: Set the player's IP address

syntax_8547.creator: Poa
syntax_8547.title: Spawn Fake Entity
syntax_8547.description:
Spawn a client side entity.

### note
These entities do NOT exist on the server. So using normal syntax's will not work on them. You cannot set variables to them. You must use packets for ever kind of manipulation on them. See the metadata packet syntax.
syntax_8547.syntax_pattern: spawn fake %entitytype% at %location% for %players% with id %number%

en:
Spawn a client side entity.

### note
These entities do NOT exist on the server. So using normal syntax's will not work on them. You cannot set variables to them. You must use packets for ever kind of manipulation on them. See the metadata packet syntax.

syntax_8539.creator: Poa
syntax_8539.title: Fake Health
syntax_8539.description: Change what the player sees for their health. Not to confuse this with health scaling, this is purely packet based

en: Change what the player sees for their health. Not to confuse this with health scaling, this is purely packet based

syntax_8544.creator: Poa
syntax_8544.title: Guardian Beam/Laser
syntax_8544.description:
Create a guardian beam for the player.

The colour is a string because I am lazy :D
*Do note, that if a colour is used, the guardian itself will glow. This sucks but cannot be made without an actual guardian. It does defeat the purpose of it but thought I would add the option anyways.*

*These are not persistent between reboots*
syntax_8544.syntax_pattern: create a guardian laser from %location% to %location% [with color %string%] with id %string% for %players%

en:
Create a guardian beam for the player.

The colour is a string because I am lazy :D
*Do note, that if a colour is used, the guardian itself will glow. This sucks but cannot be made without an actual guardian. It does defeat the purpose of it but thought I would add the option anyways.*

*These are not persistent between reboots*

syntax_8542.creator: Poa
syntax_8542.title: Fake Multi Block Change
syntax_8542.description: Similar to Skript's *send block change* effect, this can do multiple blocks within a single packet. More efficient for larger scaled faking
syntax_8542.syntax_pattern: fake multi block set %locations% as %blockdata% for %players%

en: Similar to Skript's *send block change* effect, this can do multiple blocks within a single packet. More efficient for larger scaled faking

syntax_8554.creator: Poa
syntax_8554.title: Hostname
syntax_8554.description: Similar to skript's hostname expression however, this works outside the player connect event. Making it easier to use.

en: Similar to skript's hostname expression however, this works outside the player connect event. Making it easier to use.

syntax_8548.creator: Poa
syntax_8548.title: Remove Client Entity
syntax_8548.description:
Removes an entity from a client

This works for both fake and real entities
syntax_8548.syntax_pattern: remove fake entity with id %numbers% for %players%

en:
Removes an entity from a client

This works for both fake and real entities

syntax_8546.creator: Poa
syntax_8546.title: Block In Chunk
syntax_8546.description:
Get the locations for specific blocks within a chunk

Faster than looping all blocks with Skript

*see https://pastebin.com/Fnt1xkNA for a test skript to show the performance differences*

en:
Get the locations for specific blocks within a chunk

Faster than looping all blocks with Skript

*see https://pastebin.com/Fnt1xkNA for a test skript to show the performance differences*

syntax_11264.creator: Poa
syntax_11264.title: Set Player Skin
syntax_11264.description:
Set a player's skin

### only use the signature when the `skin of` is a texture value
syntax_11264.syntax_pattern: set skin of %players% to skin of %string% [with signature %-string%]

en:
Set a player's skin

### only use the signature when the `skin of` is a texture value

syntax_8852.creator: Poa
syntax_8852.title: Fake Username ~ OUTDATED
syntax_8852.description:
Change the overhead name of the player. Not the prefix or suffix the **ACTUAL** username of the player. This is limited by the clients 16 character max characters. But it does support colour (&) *(it does use characters up though)* 

You can make it so different players see different names per person. As all packets this is not persistent.

*As of now this is only supported on* **1.19.3/4** *servers due to Mojang's mapping and ClientboundInfo packets*

en:
Change the overhead name of the player. Not the prefix or suffix the **ACTUAL** username of the player. This is limited by the clients 16 character max characters. But it does support colour (&) *(it does use characters up though)* 

You can make it so different players see different names per person. As all packets this is not persistent.

*As of now this is only supported on* **1.19.3/4** *servers due to Mojang's mapping and ClientboundInfo packets*

syntax_8540.creator: Poa
syntax_8540.title: Fake Item/Equipment
syntax_8540.description:
Make a player see your armour and holding items as different to what they are. This works for mobs too.

You can use an entity id instead of an entity for fake entities

For the string use any of the following:
**HAND**
**OFF_HAND**
**HEAD**
**CHEST**
**LEGS**
**FEET**

en:
Make a player see your armour and holding items as different to what they are. This works for mobs too.

You can use an entity id instead of an entity for fake entities

For the string use any of the following:
**HAND**
**OFF_HAND**
**HEAD**
**CHEST**
**LEGS**
**FEET**

syntax_9245.creator: Poa
syntax_9245.title: Entity ID
syntax_9245.description: Get the entity id of an entity

en: Get the entity id of an entity

syntax_9247.creator: Poa
syntax_9247.title: Player Load Entity (OUTDATED)
syntax_9247.description:
# As of PoaSK 4.16.11 this has been removed. Use SkBee's `on player track entity`

Called when a player loads an entity, not when an entity is added to a world. This is a packet event.

Useful for sending packets for things like metadata and ect.

en:
# As of PoaSK 4.16.11 this has been removed. Use SkBee's `on player track entity`

Called when a player loads an entity, not when an entity is added to a world. This is a packet event.

Useful for sending packets for things like metadata and ect.
Turn entities into BaeFell

syntax_9345.creator: Poa
syntax_9345.title: Set Camera Packet
syntax_9345.description:
Set the camera to another entity or fake entity. 
Player can rotate while looking at themselves. 
They cannot move unless riding an entity

en:
Set the camera to another entity or fake entity. 
Player can rotate while looking at themselves. 
They cannot move unless riding an entity

syntax_9335.creator: Poa
syntax_9335.title: Head Rotation Packet
syntax_9335.syntax_pattern: [send] head rotation [packet] [with] id %integer% with yaw %integer% for %players%

en:
Send the head rotation packet. 

https://wiki.vg/Protocol#Set_Head_Rotation

syntax_9356.creator: Poa
syntax_9356.title: Fake Dropped Item
syntax_9356.syntax_pattern: spawn fake [dropped] item at %location% for %players% with id %number% with [item] %itemtype%

en: Spawn a fake dropped item

syntax_9337.creator: Poa
syntax_9337.title: Rotate Entity Packet
syntax_9337.description:
Send the rotation packet for fake entities

https://wiki.vg/Protocol#Update_Entity_Rotation
syntax_9337.syntax_pattern: [send] rotate entity [packet] [with] id %integer% with yaw %integer% [and] pitch %integer% for %players%

en:
Send the rotation packet for fake entities

https://wiki.vg/Protocol#Update_Entity_Rotation

syntax_9336.creator: Poa
syntax_9336.title: Move Entity Packet
syntax_9336.description:
Move a fake entity packet.

https://wiki.vg/Protocol#Update_Entity_Position

(The amounts are not in blocks. Please read the wiki above)

# 4096 is 1 block

en:
Move a fake entity packet.

https://wiki.vg/Protocol#Update_Entity_Position

(The amounts are not in blocks. Please read the wiki above)

# 4096 is 1 block

syntax_9880.creator: Poa
syntax_9880.title: Metadata Packet (OLD)
syntax_9880.description: Set a variable to a custom raw metadata used

en: Set a variable to a custom raw metadata used

syntax_9899.creator: Poa
syntax_9899.title: Fake Entity Ride (mount)
syntax_9899.description: Make entities ride another entity but all client side. Works with fake entities
syntax_9899.syntax_pattern: make entit[ies|y] with id[s] %numbers% ride entity with id %number% for %players%

en: Make entities ride another entity but all client side. Works with fake entities
Give the riptide spin effect

syntax_9882.creator: Poa
syntax_9882.title: Send Packet
syntax_9882.description: Sends a raw packet to players

en: Sends a raw packet to players

syntax_9334.creator: Poa
syntax_9334.title: Fake Entity Name
syntax_9334.description: Fake an entities name per player.

en: Fake an entities name per player.

syntax_10884.creator: Poa
syntax_10884.title: Send action bar
syntax_10884.description: Sends an action bar packet. Similar to Skript's one however, this one won't be cancelled by the `on action bar send` event

en: Sends an action bar packet. Similar to Skript's one however, this one won't be cancelled by the `on action bar send` event

syntax_11978.creator: Poa
syntax_11978.title: Animation/Arm Swing Packet
syntax_11978.description:
Send arm swing and critical hits animation packets

### Animation ID's

0 - Arm Swing
1 - Unused by Mojang
2 - Leave Bed
3 - OffHand Swing
4 - Critical Hit
5 - Magic Critical Hit
syntax_11978.syntax_pattern: [send] animation [packet] [with] id %animation ID% for entity [with id] %number% for %players%

en:
Send arm swing and critical hits animation packets

### Animation ID's

0 - Arm Swing
1 - Unused by Mojang
2 - Leave Bed
3 - OffHand Swing
4 - Critical Hit
5 - Magic Critical Hit

syntax_10966.creator: Poa
syntax_10966.title: Fake Falling Blocks
syntax_10966.syntax_pattern: spawn fake falling block at %location% for %players% with id %number% with [block] %blockdata%

en: Spawn client sided falling blocks

syntax_10969.creator: Poa
syntax_10969.title: Connected Blocks
syntax_10969.description: Gets the connected blocks from a location that is not air

en: Gets the connected blocks from a location that is not air

syntax_11979.creator: Poa
syntax_11979.title: Player Input Packet
syntax_11979.description:
**On 1.21.3 this event is called at any input. Below this version it is only called when mounted on an entity.**

event-vector  - gives the x and z outputs of the packet. (not in 1.21.3)
event-boolean - gives whether jumping ocured (not in 1.21.3)
#### event-string - returns "W" "A" "S" "D" "SPACE" "SHIFT", "SPRINT" (note: shift will dismount, so cancel that event if needed)

*note* cancelling this event is kind of pointless since it just cancels the server from receiving the packet, but possible regardless. This will cause desync

en:
**On 1.21.3 this event is called at any input. Below this version it is only called when mounted on an entity.**

event-vector  - gives the x and z outputs of the packet. (not in 1.21.3)
event-boolean - gives whether jumping ocured (not in 1.21.3)
#### event-string - returns "W" "A" "S" "D" "SPACE" "SHIFT", "SPRINT" (note: shift will dismount, so cancel that event if needed)

*note* cancelling this event is kind of pointless since it just cancels the server from receiving the packet, but possible regardless. This will cause desync

syntax_11988.creator: Poa
syntax_11988.title: Block Change/Sent Packet
syntax_11988.description:
This event is called whenever the block update packet is sent

### WARNING
this event is fired for placing blocks, natural updates like leaves, dirt changing to grass, plant growth ect...
DO NOT CANCEL WITHOUT CHECKS

This is useful for checking custom send block changes using Skript's `make player see %block% as %block%`
syntax_11988.event_values: event-player, event-location, event-blockdata, event-block (this is the real block at the location)

en:
This event is called whenever the block update packet is sent

### WARNING
this event is fired for placing blocks, natural updates like leaves, dirt changing to grass, plant growth ect...
DO NOT CANCEL WITHOUT CHECKS

This is useful for checking custom send block changes using Skript's `make player see %block% as %block%`

syntax_11220.creator: Poa
syntax_11220.title: Remove Fake Player
syntax_11220.syntax_pattern:
remove fake player[s] with id[s] %numbers% and uuid[s] %strings% for %players%



remove fake player named %strings% for %players%   #OUTDATED

en: Remove a fake player

syntax_11221.creator: Poa
syntax_11221.title: Entity ID of fake player (OUTDATED)
syntax_11221.description: Get the entity id of a fake player sent to a player
syntax_11221.syntax_pattern: entity id of fake player named %string% with player %player%

en: Get the entity id of a fake player sent to a player

syntax_14500.creator: Poa
syntax_14500.title: All Guardian Beams
syntax_14500.description: Get a string list of all guardian beams

en: Get a string list of all guardian beams

syntax_11633.creator: Poa
syntax_11633.title: Fake player name and skin
syntax_11633.description:
Change the player's name and skin for each player.

## Note: There is a tick delay, so if the player is coming out of spectator for example, they will see the original player for a tick before it can update. This is planned on being fixed :D
syntax_11633.syntax_pattern: fake name of %player% to %string% with skin named %string% for %players%

en:
Change the player's name and skin for each player.

## Note: There is a tick delay, so if the player is coming out of spectator for example, they will see the original player for a tick before it can update. This is planned on being fixed :D

syntax_11646.creator: Poa
syntax_11646.title: Fake Lightning
syntax_11646.description: Spawn fake lightning for a player, client side lightning bolt :D

en: Spawn fake lightning for a player, client side lightning bolt :D

syntax_11671.creator: Poa
syntax_11671.title: Teleport Packet
syntax_11671.description:
Teleporting a fake entity more than 8 blocks (if less use move packet) (don't have to but is advised)

**NOTE**
*If the fake entity is out of view you have to respawn it and set all it's data again, this only works if the fake entity is within player view*

en:
Teleporting a fake entity more than 8 blocks (if less use move packet) (don't have to but is advised)

**NOTE**
*If the fake entity is out of view you have to respawn it and set all it's data again, this only works if the fake entity is within player view*

syntax_11714.creator: Poa
syntax_11714.title: System Chat Packet
syntax_11714.description:
Packet fired when chat messages are sent to a player incoming from the system. EG: broadcasts from plugins ect. 

### This is also fired on action bar

#### Note
The event-string value returns the minimessage format of the string, use `uncolored mini message from event-string` to get the raw text

en:
Packet fired when chat messages are sent to a player incoming from the system. EG: broadcasts from plugins ect. 

### This is also fired on action bar

#### Note
The event-string value returns the minimessage format of the string, use `uncolored mini message from event-string` to get the raw text

syntax_11715.creator: Poa
syntax_11715.title: Player Chat Packet
syntax_11715.description:
Packet fired when chat messages are sent to a player incoming from another player. 

#### Do not use instead of `on chat`, rather this is to stop certain people seeing messages.

#### Note
The event-string value returns the minimessage format of the string, use `uncolored mini message from event-string` to get the raw text

en:
Packet fired when chat messages are sent to a player incoming from another player. 

#### Do not use instead of `on chat`, rather this is to stop certain people seeing messages.

#### Note
The event-string value returns the minimessage format of the string, use `uncolored mini message from event-string` to get the raw text

syntax_11716.creator: Poa
syntax_11716.title: Fake Death
syntax_11716.description: Sends the death packet and the other packets required
syntax_11716.syntax_pattern: fake death [packet] of entity with id %number% for %players%

en: Sends the death packet and the other packets required

syntax_11806.creator: Poa
syntax_11806.title: Fake Entity Scale/Size
syntax_11806.description:
Fakes the scale attribute to resize entities or player

## This only works on 1.20.6+
syntax_11806.syntax_pattern: fake scale [packet] of entity with id %number% with [value|size] %number% for %players%

en:
Fakes the scale attribute to resize entities or player

## This only works on 1.20.6+

syntax_12991.creator: Poa
syntax_12991.title: Add Player To Tablist
syntax_12991.description:
Add any name and any skin with any ping to tablist.

Uses?

`hide {_target} from {_player}` removes the player from the tablist. What if you want to add them back? Now you can

#### You can use skin textures by inputting the texture into the skin name option

### BIG NOTE!!
The `with username` part is if you plan on using teams packets to sort the tablist. **DO NOT** use real player names. just make them up. This value is not shown and is used behind the scenes. For sorting only alphabetically. Example below

### Do not use real UUID's
This method spawns a fake player information without spawning the actual player entity. Using real uuids will mess things up
Keep a note of the uuid used otherwise you cannot remove them, adding 2 with the same uuid means you can only remove 1. This on the client
syntax_12991.syntax_pattern: add [player] [named] %string% to tablist [with skin named %-string%] [with latency %-number%] [with username %-string%] for %players% [with] uuid %string%

en:
Add any name and any skin with any ping to tablist.

Uses?

`hide {_target} from {_player}` removes the player from the tablist. What if you want to add them back? Now you can

#### You can use skin textures by inputting the texture into the skin name option

### BIG NOTE!!
The `with username` part is if you plan on using teams packets to sort the tablist. **DO NOT** use real player names. just make them up. This value is not shown and is used behind the scenes. For sorting only alphabetically. Example below

### Do not use real UUID's
This method spawns a fake player information without spawning the actual player entity. Using real uuids will mess things up
Keep a note of the uuid used otherwise you cannot remove them, adding 2 with the same uuid means you can only remove 1. This on the client

syntax_12992.creator: Poa
syntax_12992.title: Remove Player From Tablist
syntax_12992.description: Remove a player from tablist using UUID. Don't use this for real players *I mean you can but I would recommend the syntax from SkBee*. Use this for fake ones spawned
syntax_12992.syntax_pattern: remove tablist player[s] with uuid[s] %strings% for %players%

en: Remove a player from tablist using UUID. Don't use this for real players *I mean you can but I would recommend the syntax from SkBee*. Use this for fake ones spawned

syntax_11647.creator: Poa
syntax_11647.title: Particle Send Event
syntax_11647.description:
If you need more than the skript provides use reflect. 

Reflect Syntax's:

event.getParticle() 
event.getParticle().name()
event.setCancelled(true/false)
event.getPlayer()
event.getX()
event.getY()
event.getZ()
event.getXOffset()
event.getYOffset()
event.getZOffset()
event.getWorld()
event.getMaxSpeed()
event.getCount()



The event when a particle is sent :D



[Git Link](https://github.com/Ekpoa/PoaSkRewritev2/blob/master/nms121/src/main/java/poa/packets/packetListener/events/ParticleEvent121.java)
syntax_11647.event_values: event-player, event-particle, event-vector (offset), event-location, event-integer (amount of particles)

en:
If you need more than the skript provides use reflect. 

Reflect Syntax's:

event.getParticle() 
event.getParticle().name()
event.setCancelled(true/false)
event.getPlayer()
event.getX()
event.getY()
event.getZ()
event.getXOffset()
event.getYOffset()
event.getZOffset()
event.getWorld()
event.getMaxSpeed()
event.getCount()



The event when a particle is sent :D



[Git Link](https://github.com/Ekpoa/PoaSkRewritev2/blob/master/nms121/src/main/java/poa/packets/packetListener/events/ParticleEvent121.java)

syntax_13245.creator: Poa
syntax_13245.title: Ghost Effect (team)
syntax_13245.description:
Creates the *ghost* like effect with invisible entities


*you must have scoreboard-team anti override set to false in TAB config, if you have TAB*

en:
Creates the *ghost* like effect with invisible entities


*you must have scoreboard-team anti override set to false in TAB config, if you have TAB*

syntax_9246.creator: Poa
syntax_9246.title: Team Packet
syntax_9246.description:
Send a scoreboard team packet to player

if using players in teams, you must disable TAB anti-override.

### 1 entity/player can only be in 1 team at a time.
### If adding a player to a team, use their name and not their uuid for the uuid section

for the nametag visibility use one of the following strings (case sensitive):

always
hideForOtherTeams
hideForOwnTeam
never

for collision use one of the following strings (case sensitive):
always
pushOtherTeams
pushOwnTeam
never
syntax_9246.syntax_pattern: send team packet to %players% with name %string% [displayname %-string%] [nametag visibility %-string%] [collision %-string%] [color %-color%] [prefix %-string%]] [suffix %-string%] [and] [see friendly %-boolean%] with entity uuid %strings%

en:
Send a scoreboard team packet to player

if using players in teams, you must disable TAB anti-override.

### 1 entity/player can only be in 1 team at a time.
### If adding a player to a team, use their name and not their uuid for the uuid section

for the nametag visibility use one of the following strings (case sensitive):

always
hideForOtherTeams
hideForOwnTeam
never

for collision use one of the following strings (case sensitive):
always
pushOtherTeams
pushOwnTeam
never

syntax_13247.creator: Poa
syntax_13247.title: All items in inventories in chunk
syntax_13247.description:
If you have a chunk full of chests, this method grabs all those items

I'm adding this because the same code performed in skript froze the server for 10 seconds. In java it is a lot more performant

*note* uses for this is very little, I personally needed it, so I added it here
syntax_13247.syntax_pattern: all items in containers in chunk %chunk%

en:
If you have a chunk full of chests, this method grabs all those items

I'm adding this because the same code performed in skript froze the server for 10 seconds. In java it is a lot more performant

*note* uses for this is very little, I personally needed it, so I added it here

syntax_13304.creator: Poa
syntax_13304.title: Fake Item Cooldown
syntax_13304.description:
Fakes the cooldown of the item.

The item cooldown is similar to that used by vanilla enderpearls

This is different to the Skript's syntax because Skript's (Bukkit's) prevents the client from using the item. This does not
syntax_13304.syntax_pattern: fake item cooldown of %itemtype% for %players% for %integer% ticks

en:
Fakes the cooldown of the item.

The item cooldown is similar to that used by vanilla enderpearls

This is different to the Skript's syntax because Skript's (Bukkit's) prevents the client from using the item. This does not

syntax_13286.creator: Poa
syntax_13286.title: Set Blocks FAST
syntax_13286.description:
Uses NMS methods to set blocks fast




*just for information*
Skript crashed server trying to fill 4 million blocks
Java crashed server trying to fill 4 million blocks
This method took 0.42 seconds to fill 4 million blocks
syntax_13286.syntax_pattern:
set %locations% to %blockdata% fast  (this one is slower)
set [blocks from] %location% to %location% to %blockdata% [even] fast[er].  (this one is ALOT faster)

en:
Uses NMS methods to set blocks fast




*just for information*
Skript crashed server trying to fill 4 million blocks
Java crashed server trying to fill 4 million blocks
This method took 0.42 seconds to fill 4 million blocks

syntax_13307.creator: Poa
syntax_13307.title: Replace Blocks FAST
syntax_13307.description:
Replaces blocks within 2 locations extremely fast

Bukkit and Skript crashes the server when attempted to do 4 million blocks within a single tick

This effect takes 0.42 seconds to execute

en:
Replaces blocks within 2 locations extremely fast

Bukkit and Skript crashes the server when attempted to do 4 million blocks within a single tick

This effect takes 0.42 seconds to execute

syntax_13712.creator: Poa
syntax_13712.title: Encode / Decode Item Stack to Base64
syntax_13712.description: Turn item stacks into a base 64 expression for better storage

en: Turn item stacks into a base 64 expression for better storage

syntax_13776.creator: Poa
syntax_13776.title: world guard region
syntax_13776.description: Gets the world guard region object. This is used for reflect only

en: Gets the world guard region object. This is used for reflect only

syntax_13913.creator: Poa
syntax_13913.title: Roman Numerals
syntax_13913.description:
Convert any number to roman numerals and back again

anything above 3999 will just have a ton of M's as roman numerals have no letter for higher than this number. Minecraft doesn't support overline characters
syntax_13913.syntax_pattern:
%number% as roman numeral[s]
roman numeral[s] %string% as [a] number

en:
Convert any number to roman numerals and back again

anything above 3999 will just have a ton of M's as roman numerals have no letter for higher than this number. Minecraft doesn't support overline characters

syntax_9881.creator: Poa
syntax_9881.title: Add Data to Metadata Packet (OLD)
syntax_9881.description:
##OLD METHOD

The String options are as followed: *(capitals do not matter)*

"fire %boolean%"
"invisible %boolean%"
"glowing %boolean%"
"silent %boolean%"
"gravity %boolean%"
"name visible %boolean%"

"name %string%" *(this should follow minimessage format)*

"pose %string%" *(look at the metadata packet for list of poses)*

"health %number%"
"air %number%" (this is the remaining air, 300 is default and it is in ticks, setting it to 150 will show 5/10 bubbles that increase as you are out of the water) 

**Below is armor stand data**
"stand small %boolean%"
"stand arms %boolean%"
"stand NoBasePlate %boolean%"
"stand marker %boolean%"

"stand left arm %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand left leg %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*

"stand right arm %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand right leg %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*

"stand head %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand body %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*


"display item" with %item%
"display block" with %item%
"display interpolation delay %number%"
"display transformation duration %number%"
"display posrot %number%" *position and rotation*
"display translation %number% %number% %number%"
"display scale %number% %number% %number%"
"display left rotation %number% %number% %number% %number%"
"display right rotation %number% %number% %number% %number%"
"display billboard %string%" *fixed/vertical/horizontal/center*
"display brightness %number% %number%" *(block light, sky light)*
"display view range %number%"
"display shadow radius %number%"
"display shadow strength %number%"
"display width %number%"
"display height %number%"
"display glow override %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"display line width %integer%"
"display background color %integer% %integer% %integer% %integer%" *ARGB (no commas)*
"display text %text%"

"interaction width %number%"
"interaction height%number%"

en:
##OLD METHOD

The String options are as followed: *(capitals do not matter)*

"fire %boolean%"
"invisible %boolean%"
"glowing %boolean%"
"silent %boolean%"
"gravity %boolean%"
"name visible %boolean%"

"name %string%" *(this should follow minimessage format)*

"pose %string%" *(look at the metadata packet for list of poses)*

"health %number%"
"air %number%" (this is the remaining air, 300 is default and it is in ticks, setting it to 150 will show 5/10 bubbles that increase as you are out of the water) 

**Below is armor stand data**
"stand small %boolean%"
"stand arms %boolean%"
"stand NoBasePlate %boolean%"
"stand marker %boolean%"

"stand left arm %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand left leg %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*

"stand right arm %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand right leg %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*

"stand head %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"stand body %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*


"display item" with %item%
"display block" with %item%
"display interpolation delay %number%"
"display transformation duration %number%"
"display posrot %number%" *position and rotation*
"display translation %number% %number% %number%"
"display scale %number% %number% %number%"
"display left rotation %number% %number% %number% %number%"
"display right rotation %number% %number% %number% %number%"
"display billboard %string%" *fixed/vertical/horizontal/center*
"display brightness %number% %number%" *(block light, sky light)*
"display view range %number%"
"display shadow radius %number%"
"display shadow strength %number%"
"display width %number%"
"display height %number%"
"display glow override %number% %number% %number%" *(no commas)*
"display line width %integer%"

"interaction width %number%"

syntax_13921.creator: Poa
syntax_13921.title: New Metadata Packet
syntax_13921.description:
Metadata for entities, both for real and fake. Note that if a real entity changes pose or anything the metadata is overwritten by the server


View Example for all options

en:
Metadata for entities, both for real and fake. Note that if a real entity changes pose or anything the metadata is overwritten by the server


View Example for all options

syntax_13986.creator: Poa
syntax_13986.title: Fake Biome Packet
syntax_13986.description:
Send fake biomes. The string must be a namespace key. eg `minecraft:plains` this also works for custom biomes too

### Only for 1.12.3+

Note: sometimes you may need to send twice. send once, wait a tick and send again. Seems to be a client thing. All the code is done async.

en:
Send fake biomes. The string must be a namespace key. eg `minecraft:plains` this also works for custom biomes too

### Only for 1.12.3+

Note: sometimes you may need to send twice. send once, wait a tick and send again. Seems to be a client thing. All the code is done async.

syntax_14204.creator: Poa
syntax_14204.title: Player Action Packet
syntax_14204.description:
Called when a player runs an action

The `event-string` will return one of the following:

DROP_ITEM
ABORT_DESTROY_BLOCK
DROP_ALL_ITEMS
RELEASE_USE_ITEM
START_DESTROY_BLOCK
STOP_DESTROY_BLOCK
SWAP_ITEM_WITH_OFFHAND
syntax_14204.event_values: event-player, event-integer (sequence number), event-block (if applicable), event-location, event-string

en:
Called when a player runs an action

The `event-string` will return one of the following:

DROP_ITEM
ABORT_DESTROY_BLOCK
DROP_ALL_ITEMS
RELEASE_USE_ITEM
START_DESTROY_BLOCK
STOP_DESTROY_BLOCK
SWAP_ITEM_WITH_OFFHAND

syntax_11219.creator: Poa
syntax_11219.title: Fake Player
syntax_11219.description:
Spawns a fake player in the world. 

They do vanish on world change so run events and respawn them

### If using skin textures, change the skin name to the format of `texture,signature` or just `texture` examples below.

### If using a custom skin you just need `texture`! DO NOT USE SIGNATURE

#### Side Note: In the PoaSK config change `SaveSkinCacheToDisk: false` to `SaveSkinCacheToDisk: true` cache textures signature to disk when using just texture. Recommended for constant placing of fake characters. *If the config option is not there. Just add the line*

#### Further Note: When using just `texture` there first spawned fake entity (unless disk cached) will show no skin until the skin is fetched. Then it is cached and will be instant.
syntax_11219.syntax_pattern:
spawn fake player named %string% with skin named %string% [with name hidden %boolean%] [on tablist %boolean%] [with latency %number%] at %location% for %players% with id %number% [and] uuid %string%


spawn fake player named %string% with skin named %string% [with name hidden %boolean%] [on tablist %-boolean%] [with latency %-number%] at %location% for %players%"   #OUTDATED

en:
Spawns a fake player in the world. 

They do vanish on world change so run events and respawn them

### If using skin textures, change the skin name to the format of `texture,signature` or just `texture` examples below.

### If using a custom skin you just need `texture`! DO NOT USE SIGNATURE

#### Side Note: In the PoaSK config change `SaveSkinCacheToDisk: false` to `SaveSkinCacheToDisk: true` cache textures signature to disk when using just texture. Recommended for constant placing of fake characters. *If the config option is not there. Just add the line*

#### Further Note: When using just `texture` there first spawned fake entity (unless disk cached) will show no skin until the skin is fetched. Then it is cached and will be instant.

syntax_14321.creator: Poa
syntax_14321.title: Every Packet
syntax_14321.description:
This is an advanced feature and is **not** enabled by default. Enable it in the PoaSK config

This event is designed to be used with reflect for any packet management

This event is triggered by every packet sent to and from the client.

Heavy code under this event is not advised as it is ran on the Netty thread and could disconnect players.

### I will not give help for this event if you do not have a fundamental understanding of packets or NMS

en:
This is an advanced feature and is **not** enabled by default. Enable it in the PoaSK config

This event is designed to be used with reflect for any packet management

This event is triggered by every packet sent to and from the client.

Heavy code under this event is not advised as it is ran on the Netty thread and could disconnect players.

### I will not give help for this event if you do not have a fundamental understanding of packets or NMS

syntax_8543.creator: Poa
syntax_8543.title: Client Glow
syntax_8543.description:
Allows you to make an entity or player glow for a player and not glow for another. Or different colours. Useful for things such as teams. 

Do note: for entities (maybe players) un rendering the entity will make the player stop seeing them glow.

### If you have TAB make sure you turn `team anti-overide` to `false`
### If you do not have this option (due to TAB's newer versions) change `DisableTabTeamHandling` to `true` in PoaSK config. DO NOT CHANGE IF YOU ARE NOT USING TAB

en:
Allows you to make an entity or player glow for a player and not glow for another. Or different colours. Useful for things such as teams. 

Do note: for entities (maybe players) un rendering the entity will make the player stop seeing them glow.

### If you have TAB make sure you turn `team anti-overide` to `false`
### If you do not have this option (due to TAB's newer versions) change `DisableTabTeamHandling` to `true` in PoaSK config. DO NOT CHANGE IF YOU ARE NOT USING TAB

syntax_8535.creator: Poa
syntax_8535.title: Debug Marker (1.21.9+ has changed this. Read description)
syntax_8535.description:
Creates a Minecraft debug marker. Due to Mojang shader change at 1.16 only the green channel functions correctly upto 1.20.2

all colours work on 1.20.4 - 1.21.8

## NOTE: 1.21.9+ only green shows. Color cannot be changed, text cannot be changed it ONLY lasts for 10 seconds.
### Further note: If using via versions:
server versions lower than 1.21.9 will not show the marker to clients 1.21.9+
server versions 1.21.9+ will not show markers to clients below 1.21.9

This is all due to minecraft's changes. These packets are designed for minecraft's developers, thus not updated
syntax_8535.syntax_pattern: create debug marker at %locations% with color %color% [[and ]with alpha %-number%] [[and ]with text %-string%] for %players% [for %-timespan%]

en:
Creates a Minecraft debug marker. Due to Mojang shader change at 1.16 only the green channel functions correctly upto 1.20.2

all colours work on 1.20.4 - 1.21.8

## NOTE: 1.21.9+ only green shows. Color cannot be changed, text cannot be changed it ONLY lasts for 10 seconds.
### Further note: If using via versions:
server versions lower than 1.21.9 will not show the marker to clients 1.21.9+
server versions 1.21.9+ will not show markers to clients below 1.21.9

This is all due to minecraft's changes. These packets are designed for minecraft's developers, thus not updated

syntax_14334.creator: Poa
syntax_14334.title: Chunk Data Packet
syntax_14334.description:
This event is fired when a player loads a chunk.

## NOTE: do not make real world changes with this event. If you want to use [this](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=10460)

This event is used when faking blocks in a chunk when they load them. This is faster than using the `Player Chunk Load` and then faking blocks

Check examples of how to use

If you cancel this event, player will just see void

en:
This event is fired when a player loads a chunk.

## NOTE: do not make real world changes with this event. If you want to use [this](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=10460)

This event is used when faking blocks in a chunk when they load them. This is faster than using the `Player Chunk Load` and then faking blocks

Check examples of how to use

If you cancel this event, player will just see void
